sectionSubtitle: Eight infrastructure capabilities engineered for the reliability, scale, and intelligence that production AI systems demand.

features.title: Multi-Cloud Routing — GCP / Cloudflare / Render
features.description: HeadyCloud's orchestration layer routes workloads dynamically across Google Cloud Run (batch/CPU-intensive), Cloudflare Workers (global edge/latency-sensitive), and Render (persistent services/PostgreSQL). CSL gate logic evaluates workload semantics, current provider health, and latency SLOs to make routing decisions in under 2ms — with automatic fallback when any provider degrades.

features.title: Auto-Scaling
features.description: HeadyCloud scales from zero to production in under 3 seconds using Google Cloud Run's container-native autoscaler, augmented with φ-scaled pre-warming that predicts load spikes from traffic pattern analysis. Scale-to-zero on idle reduces infrastructure costs by up to 78% for variable workloads without sacrificing cold-start performance.

features.title: Self-Healing Mesh
features.description: The HeadyCloud service mesh continuously monitors all nodes via active health probes and passive telemetry. When a service instance fails or degrades, the mesh automatically reroutes traffic within 500ms, spins up replacement instances, and triggers root-cause analysis via the AI observability layer. No manual intervention required for 99%+ of failure scenarios.

features.title: Zero-Downtime Deploys
features.description: All deployments use a blue/green traffic shift with automated canary analysis — new versions receive 5% of traffic, with rollback triggered automatically if error rates exceed 0.1% or p99 latency increases by more than 20%. Deployment pipelines integrate with GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, and the Heady CLI for one-command production releases.

features.title: Global Edge Network
features.description: Cloudflare's 300+ point-of-presence network is first-class infrastructure in HeadyCloud, serving latency-sensitive agent interactions, static assets, and API responses with sub-10ms global p99. Edge Workers cache CSL gate states, serve pre-computed vector search results, and handle authentication — keeping origin load minimal.

features.title: Phi-Scaled Resource Allocation
features.description: HeadyCloud allocates vCPU, memory, and I/O bandwidth using golden ratio partitioning across service tiers. Critical agent inference services receive φ× the resources of background indexing jobs, which receive φ× the allocation of monitoring sidecars. This produces a naturally balanced resource hierarchy that eliminates noisy-neighbor effects without manual tuning.

features.title: Observability — OTel / Grafana
features.description: Full-stack observability is standard in every HeadyCloud deployment: OpenTelemetry traces across all service boundaries, Prometheus metrics with pre-built dashboards in Grafana, structured JSON logging to Google Cloud Logging, and AI-powered anomaly detection that surfaces actionable alerts before humans notice degradation.

features.title: 99.99% SLA Target
features.description: HeadyCloud targets four-nines uptime — 52.6 minutes of downtime per year — backed by multi-region active/active deployment, automated failover, and a self-healing mesh. The SLA covers API availability, agent runtime accessibility, and data durability. Enterprise contracts include SLA credits for any month the target is not achieved.
